structure_description: 15-section competitor-first deep dive. Front matter (cover/methodology/TOC) -> one chapter-divider opens the competitive landscape spine -> two-page executive summary anchored on market structure -> industry structure overview -> top-10 competitor matrix -> 5-year share evolution -> strategic groups map -> three deep-profile pages for the top 3 players -> pricing positioning -> M&A + partnership timeline -> disruptor watch -> methodology endnote.

sections.purpose: Report cover — title, country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag
sections.notes: Pure template fill from parsed topic metadata. Title shape: '{{country}} {{industry}} competitive landscape {{year}}'.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview of research approach: filings + trade press primary set, secondary cross-checks, KIRA estimate methodology for share splits and pricing tiers, source-tag conventions
Table of contents — generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Dark chapter break opening the report's spine — single thesis statement about the market structure (consolidating / fragmenting / contested / re-segmenting)

Snapshot of the competitive board: 4 callouts (market structure descriptor + HHI, top-3 combined share, biggest mover YoY, disruptor count). 2-col narrative anchored on the structural thesis. One chart: top-10 share bar.
sections.char_budget_hint: callout num_max=12 chars, label_max=30 chars, change-line_max=38 chars, narrative_left total_chars_max=1200

chart_type_options.name: Top-10 share bar (horizontal)
chart_type_options.use_when: Clean share % available for top 10 players — the default snapshot read

chart_type_options.name: Top-3 + rest donut
chart_type_options.use_when: Market is highly concentrated and top-3 narrative dominates the snapshot

chart_type_options.name: Top-3 vs mid-tier vs long-tail stack
chart_type_options.use_when: Tiered structure (oligopoly with fringe) is the headline read

sections.title_pattern: Five strategic implications for market participants
sections.purpose: Five implication cards keyed off the structure read — e.g. 'Position for consolidation', 'Anticipate the segment fracture', 'Hedge pricing exposure', 'Sequence entry behind the disruptor wave', 'Pace M&A windows'. Each anchored by a number from the body.
sections.char_budget_hint: implications cards body_max=360 chars each, anchor field references body data
sections.notes: This is the only de-cliented advisory voice in the report — keep it imperative-verb led.

sections.purpose: HHI index chart + fragmentation index + segment split sidebar. 4-card mini-row showing the top 4 player headlines (rank, parent, share, single recent move).
sections.char_budget_hint: sidebar body_max=280 chars, stat_lines_max=4 at 35 chars each, player mini-cards strengths_body_max=140 chars

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 keeps HHI + fragmentation chart + top 4 mini-cards. Page 2 (07_industry_structure_p2) adds a full top-12 player ranking strip with rank/parent/share/segment-focus + a deeper fragmentation commentary sidebar (mid-tier vs long-tail split).

chart_type_options.name: HHI build-up stacked bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Concentration ratio (CR4/CR8) is the core structural read

chart_type_options.name: Top-N share horizontal bar with HHI badge
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — clear share data exists for top players

chart_type_options.name: CR4 vs rest donut
chart_type_options.use_when: Highly concentrated market where CR4 share itself is the headline

sections.purpose: Table-heavy: top 10 named players with revenue (USD m or local-currency), share %, parent/ownership, segment focus, one-line recent move. Sidebar carries the methodology note for share computation.
sections.char_budget_hint: Use competitive_structure layout — 4-card row plus extended table treatment via player_cards_row scaled to two rows of 5 players each; sidebar = methodology + caveat (280 chars). Names <=45 chars, parent <=55 chars.
sections.notes: If the page renders over 720px at 10 rows, split into 08a (ranks 1-5) and 08b (ranks 6-10) — page count then becomes 16-18, still within envelope.
sections.skip_if: fewer than 6 named players identifiable from research — fall back to a 6-row matrix and flag low coverage

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 (08_competitor_matrix) holds ranks 1-5 with full table + methodology sidebar. Page 2 (08_competitor_matrix_p2) holds ranks 6-10 with the same table shape + a 'mid-tier vs leaders' contrast sidebar instead of repeating the methodology note.

chart_type_options.name: Top-10 revenue bar (inline mini)
chart_type_options.use_when: Revenue figures available for all 10 players — table-leading visual

chart_type_options.name: Share by ownership type stack
chart_type_options.use_when: Ownership mix (foreign / state / private domestic) is a structural lens

sections.purpose: 5-year stacked-area or grouped-bar chart of top-10 share movement. Narrative left column reads three structural deltas (the winner, the loser, the surprise).
sections.char_budget_hint: narrative sections_count_max=3, body_max=350 chars each. Chart data: 10 series x 5 years or top-5 split out + 'others' bucket.

chart_type_options.name: 5-year share stack (top-5 + others)
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — clean share data across 5 years for top players

chart_type_options.name: 5-year share trend lines
chart_type_options.use_when: Crossovers between specific players are the narrative — line crossings read clearer than stacks

chart_type_options.name: 3-year share trend lines
chart_type_options.use_when: Only 3 years of clean share data available (short history or recent re-segmentation)

sections.purpose: 2x2 perceptual map placing the top 10 players on two strategic axes (e.g. price tier vs distribution breadth, scale vs digital maturity, premium vs mass, integrated vs specialist). Bubble size = revenue. Narrative left identifies the four group clusters.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart is the centerpiece — title <=60 chars, subtitle <=50 chars. Narrative names the 4 quadrants and the player count in each (sections_count_max=3, body_max=350 chars each).
sections.notes: Axes chosen by the analyst from the structural read; voice_guide forbids generic 'high/low' framing — use specific strategic descriptors.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 keeps the 2x2 bubble map + 3-section narrative naming the quadrants. Page 2 (10_strategic_groups_map_p2) adds per-group deep-dive cards (4 cards, one per group) covering shared strategy, representative players, and the group's structural trajectory (consolidating / fading / emerging).

chart_type_options.name: 2x2 strategic groups bubble map
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — top players cluster cleanly on two strategic axes with revenue as size

chart_type_options.name: Quality-price scatter
chart_type_options.use_when: Strategic differentiation collapses to price-quality positioning (mature consumer category)

sections.purpose: Rank-1 player deep profile — hero block (name, tagline, 3 tags, 4 stats: revenue, share, growth, segment), 2-col body: left = positioning + recent moves; right = strengths list + watch-items.
sections.char_budget_hint: Hero stats value_max=8 chars, label_max=18 chars. Body sections body_max=380 chars each. Right col can be list (max 5 items x 90 chars) or prose.
sections.skip_if: rank-1 player has insufficient public data — substitute next player up and flag

sections.purpose: Rank-2 player deep profile — same shape as 11. Voice rule: differentiate this profile from rank-1 by leading with the strategic contrast, not duplicating the rank-1 framing.
sections.skip_if: rank-2 player has insufficient public data

sections.purpose: Rank-3 player deep profile — same shape. Often the most interesting profile (the challenger). Lead with the gap-to-#2 narrative.
sections.skip_if: rank-3 player has insufficient public data

sections.purpose: Pricing chart — typically a horizontal range bar showing each player's price band (entry/mid/premium) against a reference benchmark. Narrative reads the tier structure (e.g. 'three premium, four mid, three value') and the one or two outliers.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart title <=60 chars, subtitle <=50 chars. Narrative sections_count_max=3, body_max=350 chars each.
sections.skip_if: industry has opaque pricing (B2B custom-quoted services) — substitute a 'positioning by buyer segment' chart instead

chart_type_options.name: Quality-price scatter
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — quality proxy (rating, build tier, brand premium) is defensible alongside price

chart_type_options.name: Price-band horizontal bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Tier banding (value / mid / premium) is the cleaner read than continuous quality score

chart_type_options.name: Per-player tier-mix stack
chart_type_options.use_when: Players span multiple price tiers each (SKU mix is the story, not single position)

sections.purpose: Timeline chart of M&A deals and named partnerships in the last 24-36 months across the top 10. Narrative reads the dealmaking thesis (the consolidator, the divestor, the cross-border buyer).
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart: horizontal timeline with 8-15 events. Narrative sections_count_max=3, body_max=350 chars each.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 keeps the timeline chart + 3-section narrative (consolidator / divestor / cross-border thesis). Page 2 (15_ma_timeline_p2) adds a deal-by-deal table (acquirer, target, value, rationale, source-tag) for the 6+ events plus a sidebar reading the partnership wave separately from M&A.

chart_type_options.name: Horizontal M&A + partnership timeline
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — discrete dated events across 24-36 months

chart_type_options.name: Acquirer-target network
chart_type_options.use_when: Cross-holdings or repeat-acquirer patterns dominate (one consolidator buying multiple targets)

chart_type_options.name: Deal value bar by acquirer
chart_type_options.use_when: Deal sizes vary widely and the narrative is who is spending — value is the read, not timing

sections.title_pattern: Disruptor watch: new entrants and adjacent threats
sections.purpose: 3-6 named disruptors profiled in compact form — new entrants (<3 years), adjacent-industry threats, foreign players entering the country. Each: name, model, funding/parent, share or rev signal, threat-tier. Narrative reads the two or three credible threats vs the noise.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart slot doubles as a 6-card disruptor grid via use_case_grid-style treatment (each card ~200 chars body). Narrative sections_count_max=3, body_max=350 chars each.
sections.skip_if: no credible disruptors surfaced in research — replace with a 'demand-side substitution' read on the same page
sections.notes: Disruptor scoring is a KIRA primary call — most cards land as [primary] tagged.

chart_type_options.name: Threat-tier vs traction bubble
chart_type_options.use_when: Default — disruptors plot cleanly by threat severity vs current traction signal

chart_type_options.name: Disruptor funding bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Funding/backing is the most defensible signal (VC-heavy verticals)

chart_type_options.name: Threat heatmap (likelihood x impact)
chart_type_options.use_when: Disruptors group into a risk-matrix read rather than individual cards

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary]/[secondary]/[estimate] percentages), key data anchors (filings drawn, trade press windows, KIRA estimate methodology for share splits and pricing tiers), contact + next research footer
sections.notes: Required — buyers value transparent methodology. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report.

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '012 / NN' in page-header counter slot — total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section (player profiles 11/12/13 all carry 'Section 08 · Player profiles')
assembly_rules.divider_placement: Single dark divider opens the spine after TOC (section 04). Unlike market_analysis, no per-chapter dividers — the competitor matrix is the through-line.
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if condition triggers, omit the section AND recompute page counter for all subsequent sections. If 08_competitor_matrix splits to 08a/08b, both share section_num '05'.
